heading: Expatriation to avoid tax
label: 2107 Expatriation to avoid tax
notes: Amendments2004—Subsec. (a). reenacted heading without change and amended text of subsec. (a) generally, substituting provisions relating to imposition of tax on the transfer of the taxable estate of every decedent nonresident not a citizen of the United States if the date of death occurs during a taxable year with respect to which the decedent is subject to tax under section 877(b), for provisions relating to imposition of tax on the transfer of the taxable estate of every decedent nonresident not a citizen of the United States if, within the 10-year period ending with the date of death, such decedent lost United States citizenship, unless such loss did not have for one of its principal purposes the avoidance of taxes, and provisions describing individuals to be treated as having a principal purpose to avoid taxes.Pub. L. 108–3572001—Subsec. (c)(3). substituted “2012 and 2013” for “2011 to 2013, inclusive,”.Pub. L. 107–161997—Subsec. (c)(2)(B)(i). , substituted “such foreign country as the value of the property subjected to such taxes by such foreign country and” for “such foreign country in respect of property included in the gross estate as the value of the property”.Pub. L. 105–34, § 1602(g)(6)(A)Subsec. (c)(2)(C). , amended heading and text of subpar. (C) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “For purposes of subparagraph (B), a property’s proportionate share is the percentage of the value of the property which is included in the gross estate solely by reason of subsection (b) bears to the total value of the gross estate.”Pub. L. 105–34, § 1602(g)(6)(B)1996—Subsec. (a). , substituted “Treatment of expatriates” for “Rate of tax” in heading and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “A tax computed in accordance with the table contained in section 2001 is hereby imposed on the transfer of the taxable estate, determined as provided in section 2106, of every decedent nonresident not a citizen of the United States dying after , if after , and within the 10-year period ending with the date of death such decedent lost United States citizenship, unless such loss did not have for one of its principal purposes the avoidance of taxes under this subtitle or subtitle A.”Pub. children.heading: Treatment of expatriates
children.label: a Treatment of expatriates
children.text: A tax computed in accordance with the table contained in section 2001 is hereby imposed on the transfer of the taxable estate, determined as provided in section 2106, of every decedent nonresident not a citizen of the United States if the date of death occurs during a taxable year with respect to which the decedent is subject to tax under section 877(b).

if such decedent owned (within the meaning of section 958(a)) at the time of his death 10 percent or more of the total combined voting power of all classes of stock entitled to vote of a foreign corporation, and

children.label: A
children.text: the total combined voting power of all classes of stock entitled to vote of such corporation, or

children.label: B
children.text: the total value of the stock of such corporation,

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A credit of $13,000 shall be allowed against the tax imposed by subsection (a).

children.heading: Limitation based on amount of tax
children.label: B Limitation based on amount of tax
children.text: The credit allowed under this paragraph shall not exceed the amount of the tax imposed by subsection (a).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The tax imposed by subsection (a) shall be credited with the amount of any estate, inheritance, legacy, or succession taxes actually paid to any foreign country in respect of any property which is included in the gross estate solely by reason of subsection (b).

children.label: i
children.text: the amount which bears the same ratio to the amount of such taxes actually paid to such foreign country as the value of the property subjected to such taxes by such foreign country and included in the gross estate solely by reason of subsection (b) bears to the value of all property subjected to such taxes by such foreign country, or

children.label: I
children.text: the tax imposed by subsection (a), over

children.label: II
children.text: the tax which would be imposed by section 2101 but for this section.

children.heading: Proportionate share
children.label: C Proportionate share
children.text: In the case of property which is included in the gross estate solely by reason of subsection (b), such property’s proportionate share is the percentage which the value of such property bears to the total value of all property included in the gross estate solely by reason of subsection (b).

children.heading: Other credits
children.label: 3 Other credits
children.text: The tax imposed by subsection (a) shall be credited with the amounts determined in accordance with subsections (a) and (b) of section 2102. For purposes of subsection (a) of section 2102, sections 2012 and 2013 shall be applied as if the credit allowed under paragraph (1) were allowed under section 2010.

children.heading: Burden of proof
children.label: d Burden of proof
children.text: If the Secretary establishes that it is reasonable to believe that an individual’s loss of United States citizenship would, but for this section, result in a substantial reduction in the estate, inheritance, legacy, and succession taxes in respect of the transfer of his estate, the burden of proving that such loss of citizenship did not have for one of its principal purposes the avoidance of taxes under this subtitle or subtitle A shall be on the executor of such individual’s estate.

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: e Cross reference
children.text: For comparable treatment of long-term lawful permanent residents who ceased to be taxed as residents, see section 877(e).
